old_claim.note: Molecular Diagnostics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: RPM depends on patient-generated physiological data from ambulatory or wearable monitoring devices, not on molecular diagnostic assays.

source_shape.source_locator: RPM meta-analysis definition describing wearable devices that automatically transmit data to portals or mobile apps.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review defines remote patient monitoring around non-invasive wearable devices transmitting patient data for self-monitoring, provider assessment, and clinical decision-making.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports ambulatory monitoring as the correct predecessor while rejecting molecular diagnostics because RPM centers on transmitted physiological measurements rather than nucleic-acid or protein assays.

ontology_before: Remote patient monitoring depended on molecular diagnostics, conflating remote monitoring workflows with laboratory molecular testing.
ontology_after: Remote patient monitoring now depends on ambulatory physiological monitoring plus connected telehealth infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: molecular diagnostics is absent and ambulatory physiological monitoring is the enabling patient-data foundation.
would_reject_if: The RPM node were narrowed to remote molecular test result reporting rather than patient monitoring.
A source showed remote patient monitoring requires molecular diagnostics as part of the method.
The graph removed connected data transmission while replacing the molecular diagnostic edge.
short_reason: RPM monitors transmitted physiological data; molecular diagnostics is the wrong field parent.
